title: CoStar Group, Inc. (CSGP) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of CoStar Group, Inc (CSGP) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 15.0% growth rate reflects CoStar's incredibly strong, high-margin commercial real estate SaaS business and its massive ongoing expansion into the residential portal market with Homes.com. The transition of Homes.com from a heavy investment phase to monetization is expected to drive substantial free cash flow acceleration.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.0% discount rate balances the company's near-monopoly status and recurring revenue in the commercial space against the fierce, expensive competition it faces as it attempts to disrupt entrenched players like Zillow in the residential market.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 3.5% is a robust terminal rate for a digital monopoly. Assuming CoStar successfully cements Homes.com alongside its commercial and multifamily assets, it will benefit from immense, highly scalable, two-sided network effects in perpetuity.

thesisParagraphs: CoStar Group has long dominated the commercial real estate data market, building a proprietary database that functions as an irreplaceable utility for brokers and investors. This core business throws off immense, predictable cash flow.

The true upside lies in its aggressive pivot to the consumer residential market. By heavily investing to scale Homes.com—and leveraging the proven playbook it used with Apartments.com—CoStar is attempting a hostile takeover of a massive new TAM. The current valuation appears to penalize the company excessively for its near-term marketing spend while underestimating the long-term cash flow potential of establishing a dominant, integrated real estate portal.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 15.0% growth rate for CoStar?
faq.answer: Gemini anticipates that the massive upfront marketing investments in Homes.com will eventually taper, while the platform begins to monetize heavily. This, combined with the steady, high-margin growth of its core commercial suite, warrants an aggressive cash flow projection.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for CoStar's DCF?
faq.answer: A 9.0% discount rate was selected. While the commercial business is incredibly stable, the residential expansion carries significant execution risk and requires competing against formidable, well-capitalized incumbents like Zillow.

faq.question: Is it safe to rely on AI for stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice. AI models cannot predict regulatory actions, geopolitical shifts, or black swan economic events.
